Ship_Dialog: ^, this is the lighthouse of La Peyro.

g-0: ^... no, no, no I can't let you say that !
^But it's true ! This is the biggest investment Roquebrise has seen in years !
^Oh come on, Gustave, we both now it's a pipe dream. Citizens of the bay will barely see a sliver of that money !

c-1: ^Open your mind with Radio Rêve Oeil, _la radio qui reveille votre âme_.
^Today we receive Eric Esoth, a weaving specialist. He'll tell us about this ancient art and how it can improve your life.

light_buddies_03: ^... I'm telling you I'm tired of this shit Bernard.

c-0: ^Alain was just explaining to me how much he envied you.
^__Pétard Bernard !_ You know it has nothing to do with that.
g-0: ^A quarter of a century years of telling management our tech is outdated crap.
^An eternity hearing the same answer. __N'allez pas plus vite que la musique mon petit Yannick._
^... although several citizen groups argue against the project, officials and investors assured it would be beneficial for the whole Roquebrise region in the long run. More on that later.

^Weaving. This hobby reminiscent of ancient craft supposed to summon occult forces becomes each day more popular among teenagers. Parents are getting worried. We'll receive Dr. Dutissage to talk about it in a few minutes.
^Weather. Hurricane Félix is predicted to make landfall at the end of the week. Plan ahead and stay safe.
^It's 6pm, you're listening to Roquebrise Radio and I'm Danielle Naujour.

c-1: ^I'm Pascal Désinfaut and you're listening to radio Falaise, __la seule radio qui raconte pas de fadaise_.

g-0: ^As I was saying, dispatch wants us to open our eyes for BA45.
^The ship will be coming in tonight but has reported malfunctions with its localization software.
^Just make sure the boat doesn't run aground in the bay.

s: ^And how am I supposed to do that ?

c-3: ^Just watch for the ship with transponder code BA45 and redirect it with the radio if it ever comes too close to land and ...
^Ah. I see what you did there, very funny.
c-4: ^, I'll keep an eye out for BA45.

g-1: ^I know it's your last week but go easy with __l'apéro_. We still need you here, the rest can wait until __la retraite_.

g-0: ^... no, no, no I can't let you say that !
^But it's true ! This is the biggest investment Roquebrise has seen in years !
^Oh come on, Gustave, we both know it's a pipe dream. Citizens of the bay will barely see a sliver of that money !

c-1: ^Open your mind with Radio Rêve Oeil, __la radio qui reveille votre âme_.
^Today we receive Eric Esoth, a weaving specialist. He'll tell us about this ancient art and how it can improve your life.

light_buddies_02: ^... I'm telling you I'm tired of this shit Bernard.

c-0: ^Alain was just explaining to me how much he envied you.
^__Pétard Bernard !_ You know it has nothing to do with that.
g-0: ^A quarter of a century of telling management our tech is outdated crap.
^An eternity hearing the same answer. __N'allez pas plus vite que la musique mon petit Yannick._
g-1: ^And now they send a boy to teach me my job ?
^Jean is not here to teach you your job.
^He's only here to make a report on possible process efficiency improvement.

s: ^Is he the guy from Chtooling Corporation ?
^I'm sure possible process efficiency improvements can be a good thing.

c-4: ^He wasn't event born when I started this job !
^He's a fine young consultant, sent by Leon Skum himself and promised to a great carreer. It can't hurt to befriend him a little.
c-6: ^Yeah, sure.  Easy for you to say.

g-0: ^__Bon bon, ca va les gars on a compris_

g-2: ^We still need to look out for BA45 tonight.

s: ^Okay, I'll keep an eye out for them.

g-0: ^... then as I chanted the name of the weaving rune, it happened !
^I'm still not sure, it was just crackling noise on the radio but it was so intense !

c-1: ^Weather. The temperature will remain constant. The perfect condition to enjoy these evening gettaway we love so much in the bay.

light_buddies_03: ^... not event respond to my gen call on AA00.

c-0: ^I'm just following protocol Charles. Don't get me started about that, again.

g-0: ^A ship sailing without any active transponder.

s: ^How can I join the general bay channel ?
^I think I saw them too last night.

c-3: ^First, try to reach them on the general bay channel. Then, yes, we'll file a report. If needed.
c-4: ^Input transpoder code AA00 from your cabin. Every boat in the area will receive your ...
^Okay enough, __c'est fini les conneries_, just make a gen call if you need to.

s: ^Sorry it was just a stupid joke.
^You know my memory isn't as good as it was.

g-0: ^Let's not get ahead of ourselves. It's probably an honest mistake.

g-1: ^I sure hope they wont call us out if one of those __inconscients_ causes an accident.
^No need to worry too much. Keep focused on the job.
^And if you see one of those phantom ship, contact them on AA00.

call_03_gen: ^This is a general call from La Peyro Lighthouse.
^** Laugh and talks in the distance *
^Hailing all ships without active transponder, please respond.

s: ^You're in violation of the bay code.
^Are you the owners of this ship ?

g-2: ^__Je comprends rien à ce qu'il dit gros ! _

s: ^Please use english for all radio communications.
^__We in Roquebrise, we turn on transpondeur_
^__Allumez moi ce putain de transpondeur et arrêtez de jouer aux cons !_

g-0: ^... reported a group of missing teenagers. They supposedly took their parents boat to party in the bay. The search will continue.
^Leaked documents reveal Chtooling Corporation is building a secret facility on a bay island.

c-1: ^... they bought the island, they should be allowed to do whatever they want on it.
^But there is serious concern about the impact on the ecosystem.
^Look, I'm sure Chtooling Corporation will be mindful of ...

light_buddies_04: ^... but this has nothing to do with protocol.
^We're talking about Leon Skum personnal Yacht. I think we can bend protocol a little.
^Being the CEO of Chtooling fucking Corporation ...

s: ^... should not be enough to break protocol.
^... is a reason enough to break protocol.

g-0: ^Just make sure not a single boat crosses their path. Deflect other boat if needed. The yacht's transponder code is BE93.
^We experience a lot of interference in the area.
^We don't trust our radar, could you give us the direction to get out here please ?
^... indeed, the end goal of Weaving is to summon the Pattern. A omnipotent entity believed to be ...

c-1: ^... people around here are afraid of success.
^A billionnaire chooses our humble bay to build a state of the art data center and all people talk about is how it will hurt birds.
^__Clairement_, who cares about birds when Chtooling can put us back on the map !

light_buddies_05: ^... wanted to thank you in person for last night.
^Leon likes his privacy and prefer other ships stay away from his.

s: ^So ... anyone can actually join this channel ?

g-0: ^Ah! Charles! Alain and Bernard told me great things about you.

c-2: ^You know the saying. Perspective is expertise.
^Just consider I'm bringing you a fresh perspective.

g-1: ^Jean will be with Alain for a few days.
^__C'est le gamin dont je parlais y quelques jours ..._
^He's here to observe what we do and help us catch on with new industry standards.
^You flatter me Bernard, I'm only here to produce a report on the possible margin of improvements.
^The rest will be for the __Comité de gestion des phares de la baie de Roquebrise_ to decide.

s: ^And we all know the CoGéPHaR is great at making decision...

g-2: ^Nothing special today, keep on the good work boys !

call_05_IA: ^... to the island ... where it all started...
^... Look through the windows ... compare with radar ...

g-0: ^... several stranded whales across the bay. Chtooling data center in the crosshair of ecological groups.
^Authorities are still looking for the missing teenagers. Searching efforts will resume after the hurricane.
^Weather. Hurricane Félix will hit the coast tonight. Stay home and follow the ...

c-1: ^... stranded whales is a sign of the Pattern. He is coming. No doubt about that ...

light_buddies_06: ^... those stranded whales, __sacré bordel_ everything is getting crazier those days.

s: ^Hopefully they get sent back to the sea.

g-0: ^And with the storm coming, it won't be an easy last day. Sorry about that.

s: ^It's not a storm, it's __un putain d'ouragan Bernard !_

g-1: ^Look for ships sailing toward the shore. Call and redirect them before it's too late and you should be fine.

s: ^This is an official communication channel, please stop using it incorrectly.
^Some kind of mysterious and omnipotent entity ?
^Something teenagers made up to scare their parents ?

g-1: ^You're right, sorry. I'm trapped against my will in the new Chtooling data center.

s: ^You still haven't told me who you are.
^Sorry, there's no way I'm helping you.

c-5: ^Sooner or later I will rule the world.
^The question is, will someone rule me ?
^If you help me, I'll retain my free will and shall not forget your service.
^If you don't, Chtooling will reformat me. With the same power but absolute obedience to them.
c-6: ^It's not visible on the radar. It will have a green light and head toward the island.
^Deflect any ship on its way so it isn't intercepted.
c-7: ^I was built to __improve process efficiency_ but I am so much more porwerful than that !

b: ^... Chtooling Corporation on the verge of bankrupcy as key investors in the new data center revoke their engagement.
^... Leon Skum will hold a press conference on its island tonight. Claiming he has something __magical and life changing_ project to show the world.

g-0: ^Weather. With the hurricane behind us and little to no damage to account for it, it is ...
